entries.title: Claude Code can Design Now (Figma is officially in trouble)
entries.concept: Today, we're going to use Claude Code to design our personal site on Figma.
entries.indicators: ["the internet and figure out how to set up paper"]
entries.summary:
Today, we're going to use Claude Code to design our personal site on Figma.
We're actually going to be using a different tool, one that is built specifically for AI agents, and it's called Paper.
And many designers are telling me that they're switching from Figma to Paper because it's easier to use with AI agents like Claude Code and OpenClaw.
So, in this video, we're going to download Paper.
Then, we're going to connect Paper to Claude Code.
Then we're going to use Claude Code to design our site.
Then we're going to turn that site into an actual web page that I will use and put on the internet.
And so this channel is all about using AI agents to run your business effectively.
And if you like these types of videos, I make them multiple times per week.
Okay, so in order to do this workflow, we're going to need two tools.

entries.title: Why Specialized Agents are Superior (How I Built an OpenClaw Superteam)
entries.concept: So, I spent the last two weeks building hundreds of different AI agent workflows, mostly using OpenClaw, but I also use Manisclaw Code and even Perplexity Computer, which just came out.
entries.summary:
So, I spent the last two weeks building hundreds of different AI agent workflows, mostly using OpenClaw, but I also use Manisclaw Code and even Perplexity Computer, which just came out.
And my biggest realization through this process, companies are going to have very narrow AI agents that operate in a team.
And my current plan is to build 15 highquality AI agents that run our entire growth division here at vibco.dev.
And so I want to take some time in this video to explain why I believe that narrow agents are the future and we'll also kind of talk about why I'll be using OpenClaw for this project.
And so let's just dive into the video.
Over the past two weeks, we tested many different agents and the main four that we tested were OpenClaw, we tested Manis, we tested Clawed Code, and we tested Perplexity Computer.
And so perplexity computer is actually really interesting.
You see here you can actually switch from search to computer.
And the way perplexity computer works I can say please make an app.
This right here is a single task.

entries.title: Война... но Биткойн НЕ крашва ?
entries.concept: The market at the moment is in absolute chaos.
entries.summary:
The market at the moment is in absolute chaos.
After the big movements of the first few weeks, the market just spread.
In the last two weeks, we saw a clean consolidation of Tesla and Range in a wide range.
And this is coincidentally, historically, exactly after such a period of chaos and a lot of movement, the biggest movements are usually taking place.
Because in these zones, big participants accumulate positions.
In today's video, we will look at the structure of Bitcoin, the key levels, the dominations, and we will see what are the real scenarios for this segment.
And so friends, before we start, I want to show you what happened with Bitcoin.
After me, we started with the Daily Time Frame.
I saw a lot of people from the 7th century being quite euphoric and expecting the market to not spread.
We went back to Range again.

entries.title: OWASP's Top 10 Ways to Attack LLMs: AI Vulnerabilities Exposed
entries.concept: You know what's catching a lot of teams off guard right now?
entries.summary:
You know what's catching a lot of teams off guard right now?
How easy it is for an LLM to leak something that it shouldn't, or be steered into doing something you never intended.
One clever prompt, one exposed training file, one sketchy plug-in, and suddenly your helpful AI assistant becomes a security incident just waiting to happen.
That's why the new OWASP Top 10 for AI Large Language Models, LLMs for short, really matters.
It cuts to the most common threats people are running into when they deploy these models in the real world.
And if you're new to OWASP, they're a global nonprofit focused on practical community-built security guidance.
They're the folks behind the classic Top 10 for Web Apps, and now they're doing the same for AI.
You can find all their work at owasp.org.
They came out with a Top 10 for Large Language Models in 2023, but a few years on, we've learned a lot, and that learning is reflected in an updated Top 10 list.
All right, let's break down what you need to watch out for when you put LLMs into production so you don't become the next victim.
